description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a ribbed texture, likely intended to provide traction or drainage. The block is solid and substantial, with a dark, mottled stone appearance that suggests durability and resistance to weathering. The sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The coloration is predominantly dark gray to black, with subtle variations in tone that mimic natural stone. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments as a barrier or obstacle, often found in areas requiring both strength and a non-slip surface.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a ribbed, textured appearance. The sides are smooth and the coloration is a mottled dark gray to black.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the horizontal grooves running across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the vertical grooves, making the ribbed texture more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the flat face and horizontal grooves on the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and ribbed texture.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or tactile paving element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with adjacent pavement or blocks
